title: senna spinescens var. spinescens
intro: Senna spinescens var. spinescens, also known as the spiny senna, is a variety of plant in the legume family that is native to Australia and parts of Asia. It is a shrub-like plant with spiny stems and leaves, and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its laxative and anthelmintic effects. The leaves and pods of the senna spinescens var. spinescens contain anthraquinone glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and can help to stimulate bowel movements, reduce inflammation, and improve digestion, providing relief from constipation and other gastrointestinal issues. In herbal medicine, the plant is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health problems, including constipation, diarrhea, and digestive disorders.
